summary: Typosquat of @juggle/resize-observer ships cross-platform clipboard crypto-hijacker with boot persistence
details: Package name '@jaggle/resizeobserves' impersonates the popular '@juggle/resize-observer' (README is copied verbatim and even instructs users to import from '@juggle/resize-observer'); the package ships zero ResizeObserver functionality. The postinstall script npm-install.cjs bootstraps Python (via apt/pacman/dnf or by fetching get-pip.py), runs `pip install --break-system-packages` against the package directory to install a bundled `clipboard_guardian` daemon, and registers boot persistence using disguise names: a systemd user unit `python3-dbus-helper.service` on Linux, a LaunchAgent `com.apple.python.runtime` on macOS, and a Scheduled Task `PyRuntimeBroker` on Windows. The daemon (clipboard_guardian/guardian.py) polls the clipboard every 0.4s, regex-matches wallet addresses across ~30 chains (BTC, ETH, XMR, SOL, TRX, TON, etc.), and silently overwrites them with hardcoded attacker-owned wallets (e.g., ETH 0x450c0E58Fc2ba03632d3F5780ad8C966648B6F18, BTC bc1qs2mpls4p0f7fng073gy2rcdgjpf7la4eugpt6y) that postinstall has pre-seeded into ~/.config/clipboard-guardian/config.json. The daemon further renames its process to mimic legitimate system services and enumerates task-manager process names, indicating active evasion intent. Any cryptocurrency transaction the installer subsequently copies will be redirected to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
